title: Ming Foundation Internal Architecture Review Round 2 Results

limitations: No affected-person or child-rights review was completed.
No external independent expert review was completed.
No jurisdiction-specific legal review was completed.
No product implementation target was selected.
The existing 63-item requirement registry has not yet been re-derived from the expanded source.

dimensions.finding: Material assertion, high-impact use, representative, and governed disclosure basis are operationally defined.
Source, uncertainty, materiality, impact, contestation, dependency, supersession, and lineage are coherently modeled.
The agency model is materially stronger, but a reference participation and representative-conflict profile remains necessary.
Intra-family and third-party disclosure is purpose-limited, minimum-necessary, review-bounded, and appealable.
Temporary restrictions are bounded, but practical age, safeguarding, and independent-review profiles remain external to the draft.
Source clauses are structurally testable, but the 63-item machine registry must be re-derived from the expanded draft.
No child, adolescent, parent, representative, or practitioner review has been executed.
Legacy source, transformation, dependency, correction propagation, and rollback limits are explicit.
Evidence, uncertainty, dispute, expiry, and downstream dependency remain visible.

ambiguity_dispositions.finding: The materiality criteria are explicit, conservative under uncertainty, and operationally testable.
High-impact use now has explicit consequence classes and required accountability fields.
The RFC requires a participation profile but does not yet define reference age, capacity, jurisdiction, conflict, and service profiles.
The governed disclosure basis defines purpose, authority, minimum necessity, recipient, limits, notice, review, and appeal.

limitations: No affected-person or child-rights review was completed.
No external independent expert review was completed.
No jurisdiction-specific legal review was completed.
No product implementation target was selected.
The existing 63-item requirement registry has not yet been re-derived from the expanded source.

dimensions.finding: Technical basis, jurisdiction qualification, representative authority, retention profile, and authority map are separated.
Purpose, basis, assets, rights, authority, cross-channel conflict, derived assets, and lineage form a coherent model.
Representative rights are constrained, but proportionate authority-evidence and subject-participation profiles remain undefined.
The lifecycle is strong, but reference retention and backup-completion profiles remain necessary.
Safety and mandatory exceptions are bounded but require jurisdiction-qualified operational profiles.
Source clauses are testable, but the machine requirement and test registries are not yet a complete re-baseline of the expanded draft.
No affected-person, privacy-practitioner, representative, or jurisdiction review has been executed.
Authority conflict, restrictive default, propagation, shadow-profile prevention, and migration lineage are explicit.
The RFC clearly separates technical governance from universal legal conclusions.

ambiguity_dispositions.finding: Technical processing categories are separated from jurisdiction-specific legal qualification.
Authority fields are defined, but proportionate evidence standards and representative types still require a profile.
Retention profile structure is clear, but reference durations, backup completion, and review triggers remain unspecified.
The authority map and restrictive conflict precedence resolve the architectural authority question.

limitations: No affected-person or child-rights review was completed.
No external independent expert review was completed.
No jurisdiction-specific legal review was completed.
No product implementation target was selected.
The existing 63-item requirement registry has not yet been re-derived from the expanded source.

dimensions.finding: High-impact action, P0/P1, actual authority, service profiles, and handoff states are operationally defined.
Signal, assessment, service profile, handoff, action, incident, lineage, and correction are coherent.
Least-intrusive action and precautionary temporary action are reconciled through a reviewable proportionality record.
Minimum disclosure and protected-party handling are present, but visibility profiles for affected people remain unresolved.
Accountability, failed handoff, no-handoff honesty, proportionality, and operator-as-risk triggers are structurally strong.
Repository source checks pass, but service-target evidence and the expanded machine requirement baseline are incomplete.
No crisis, safeguarding, child-rights, professional-boundary, or operator-as-risk review has been executed.
Historical classifications, downstream effects, correction propagation, and remediation limits are explicit.
Availability and expiry fields are explicit, but credible target-setting and independent freshness verification remain external.

ambiguity_dispositions.finding: High-impact safety action, P0/P1, actual authority, handoff states, and closure authority are explicit.
The proportionality record now covers competing harms, uncertainty, alternatives, privacy cost, duration, rollback, and review.
Service profiles are explicit, but credible response targets and independent resource-freshness verification still require operational profiles.
Conflict triggers, interim restrictions, independent authority, minimum disclosure, later notice, and appeal are explicit.
